title: Senior Badminton Ranking Tournament Returns to Tamil Nadu After Five Years
indian_angle: The return of this major domestic event to Tamil Nadu after five years is significant for Indian badminton players and fans.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is set to be held in Chennai from July 16-23, marking its return to the state after a five-year hiatus.

title: Player Eligibility Announced for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: This circular outlines the crucial eligibility criteria for Indian junior players aiming to represent the nation at the upcoming World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: Badminton Association of India has released guidelines regarding player eligibility for selection to the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, scheduled to be held in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships Needs Gen Z, Gen Alpha Watchers for Badminton's Future
indian_angle: With India hosting the BWF World Championships in Delhi, there's a focus on engaging younger generations to ensure the sport's long-term popularity in the country.
summary: An article discusses the importance of attracting Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences to badminton, especially with the BWF World Championships 2026 being hosted in Delhi, to secure the sport's future fanbase.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Implemented for Domestic Ranking Tournaments and National Championships
indian_angle: The new 3x15 scoring system will significantly change how domestic ranking tournaments and national championships are played in India, impacting player strategies.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships across U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, debuting at Ernakulam.

title: Indian Shuttlers Must Learn to Think Independently, Win Without Constant Coach Presence
indian_angle: This opinion piece critically examines the development of Indian shuttlers, advocating for greater self-reliance and strategic thinking on court without constant coaching intervention.
summary: An opinion piece suggests that Indian badminton players need to develop more independent thinking and on-court decision-making skills to succeed, rather than relying heavily on coaches during matches.
